name: Asian Pasta Salad
instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a rolling boil over high heat. Add the spaghetti and cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente (tender but firm to the bite), about 10 to 12 minutes. Drain through a colander and rinse thoroughly under cold running water to stop the cooking. Transfer the cooled pasta to a large serving bowl and toss with the olive oil to prevent sticking.

2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, white sugar, rice vinegar, toasted sesame seeds, sweet chili sauce, and sesame oil until the sugar dissolves completely and the dressing is smooth.

3. Pour the soy sauce dressing over the pasta and toss well to coat evenly. Add the chopped green onion, diced red bell pepper, and sugar snap peas, gently tossing to distribute the vegetables throughout.

4. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to overnight to allow the flavors to fully meld and develop. Toss again just before serving to refresh.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook spaghetti until al dente, rinse with cold water, and toss with oil.

2. Whisk together soy sauce, sugar, vinegar, sesame seeds, chili sauce, and sesame oil until dissolved.

3. Toss pasta with dressing, then add green onions, bell pepper, and snap peas.

4. Chill for at least 30 minutes to let flavors meld, toss before serving!
